headline: Health Literacy Survey Shows 56% of Adults Cannot Interpret Labels
context: MOH health literacy assessment found majority of adults struggle to understand medicine labels and nutritional information.

cards.big: 56% of Malaysian adults scored inadequate on health literacy assessment in 2025
cards.sub: Inability to understand health information leads to medication errors and poor choices

cards.big: Medicine label comprehension was lowest among B40 adults at 34% adequate literacy
cards.sub: Education and income gaps directly translate to health information gaps

cards.big: Low health literacy costs Malaysia an estimated RM4.8 billion in avoidable healthcare use
cards.sub: Patients with poor health literacy visit emergency departments 3 times more often

cards.big: Austria's health literacy curriculum reaches 2.4 million adults through community programs since 2019
cards.sub: Targeted non-school programs effectively improve adult health decision-making

cards.big: A health system that communicates in language patients cannot understand is failing its basic duty
Should health literacy programs be integrated into community centers and workplaces nationally
